title: bryophyllum ambrense
intro: Bryophyllum ambrense, also known as the Madagascar succulent, is a type of plant species belonging to the Crassulaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues. The herbal preparations of Bryophyllum ambrense can be consumed as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in topical applications to promote skin health and wound healing.
intro_study: According to "Pharmaceutical biology", bryophyllum ambrense benefits include its potential in inhibiting Helicobacter pylori growth and acting as an antioxidant to protect gastric mucosa against reactive oxygen species.
